programs.name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
programs.description: Monthly grocery benefit loaded onto an EBT card

monthly_benefit_range.note: Amount depends on income, expenses, and household size

income.note: Federal Poverty Level 2026. Use household size to calculate limit.

asset_limits.note: Vehicles, primary home, and retirement accounts generally excluded

eligibility_rules.household_requirements: Must prepare and eat meals together

application.expedited_eligibility: Households with less than $150/month gross income AND less than $100 in resources qualify for benefits within 7 days

required_documents: Photo ID (driver's license, passport, or state ID)
Proof of address (utility bill, lease, or mail)
Proof of income for all household members (pay stubs, employer letter)
Social Security numbers for all household members applying
Proof of rent or mortgage if claiming shelter deduction
Proof of childcare expenses if applicable
Proof of medical expenses for elderly or disabled household members

programs.name: Medicaid
programs.description: Free or low-cost health insurance for low-income individuals and families

monthly_benefit_range.note: No premium in most states for qualifying individuals. Value is the health coverage itself — typically worth $3,000-$8,000/year per person.

income.note: Income limits vary by state and population group. 138% FPL applies in expansion states.

eligibility_rules.expansion_states: 39 states + DC have expanded Medicaid to adults up to 138% FPL. Non-expansion states have much stricter limits.
eligibility_rules.asset_limits: No asset test for ACA Medicaid expansion population

application.retroactive_coverage: Can cover up to 3 months before application date

programs.name: CHIP
programs.description: Low-cost or free health coverage for children in families that earn too much for Medicaid but cannot afford private insurance

monthly_benefit_range.note: Premiums are typically $0-$50/month depending on income. Covers doctor visits, prescriptions, dental, vision.

income.note: Many states extend to 300% or higher. Check state-specific file.

programs.name: LIHEAP
programs.full_name: Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program
programs.description: Helps pay heating and cooling bills. Can also help with energy-related emergencies.

monthly_benefit_range.note: One-time seasonal payment, not monthly. Average benefit is $500/year.

income.note: States can set limits as high as 60% of state median income

eligibility_rules.priority: Households with elderly, disabled, or young children get priority

application.where: Local Community Action Agency — find at liheapch.acf.hhs.gov
application.note: Funds run out quickly — apply early in the season (Oct-Nov for heating)

required_documents: Proof of home ownership or lease

programs.name: WIC
programs.full_name: Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children
programs.description: Food, healthcare referrals, and nutrition education for pregnant women, new mothers, and children under 5

monthly_benefit_range.note: Benefit is a food package (formula, milk, eggs, cereal, juice, etc.), not cash. Approximate cash value is $30-$60/month per person.

eligibility_rules.categorical_eligibility: Automatic if already on Medicaid or SNAP

application.where: Local WIC office — find at wic.fns.usda.gov/local-agencies

required_documents: Proof of income OR proof of participation in Medicaid/SNAP
Child's birth certificate (if applying for child)

programs.name: SSI
programs.description: Monthly cash payments for people who are elderly (65+), blind, or disabled, with limited income and resources

monthly_benefit_range.note: 2026 federal maximum $967/individual, $1,450/couple. Many states add a supplement.

income.note: Complex calculation. Earned income is excluded up to $65/month + half of remainder. Many types of income are fully excluded.

eligibility_rules.citizenship: Must be US citizen OR qualified alien

application.where: ssa.gov or local Social Security office

required_documents: Birth certificate or proof of age
Proof of citizenship or immigration status
Medical records if applying for disability
Proof of income and resources (bank statements, pay stubs)

programs.name: TANF
programs.description: Temporary cash assistance and services for low-income families with children

monthly_benefit_range.note: Highly variable by state. Some states provide very low benefits. Typically $300-500/month for a family of 3.

eligibility_rules.who: Families with children under 18 (or 19 if in school)

income.note: Varies dramatically by state. Generally must be below 50-85% FPL.

eligibility_rules.citizenship: Must be citizen or qualified immigrant. Children who are citizens may qualify even if parents are not.
eligibility_rules.time_limit: Federal 60-month lifetime limit. States can set stricter limits.
eligibility_rules.work_requirements: Most recipients must work, participate in job training, or perform community service

application.where: State welfare or human services office

required_documents: Social Security numbers for all household members
Proof of childcare costs if applicable

programs.name: ACA Health Insurance Subsidies
programs.full_name: Affordable Care Act Premium Tax Credits
programs.description: Tax credits that reduce monthly health insurance premiums for people who buy coverage through the marketplace

monthly_benefit_range.note: Amount varies widely based on income, age, location, and plan chosen. Average subsidy is ~$500/month.

income.note: Enhanced subsidies available through at least 2025 make coverage free for incomes up to 150% FPL

application.open_enrollment: November 1 - January 15 (special enrollment for life events)

required_documents: Estimated annual income for coverage year

programs.name: Lifeline
programs.description: Up to $9.25/month discount on phone or internet service. $34.25/month for households on Tribal lands.

eligibility_rules.categorical_eligibility: Automatic if on SNAP, Medicaid, SSI, Federal Public Housing Assistance, or Veterans Pension/Survivor's Benefit

required_documents: Proof of income OR proof of participation in qualifying program

programs.name: Section 8 Housing Voucher
programs.description: Rental assistance that helps pay the difference between your income and fair market rent

monthly_benefit_range.note: Generally covers the portion of rent above 30% of household income, up to the area's fair market rent.

income.note: 75% of new vouchers must go to households below 30% AMI

application.wait_list: WARNING: Most areas have waiting lists of 2-10 years. Apply to every open waiting list in your area.

required_documents: Birth certificates for all household members

programs.name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
programs.description: Refundable tax credit for low-to-moderate income workers. Worth up to $8,046/year for families with 3+ children.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Annual credit divided by 12. Amount depends on income, filing status, and number of qualifying children. 2026 maximum: $8,046 (3+ children), $7,152 (2 children), $4,328 (1 child), $649 (no children).

income.note: Must have earned income. Investment income limit $11,600. Gross income limit (single/HH): ~$59,899 (3+ kids), ~$55,768 (2 kids), ~$49,084 (1 kid), ~$18,591 (no kids). Add ~$6,960 for married filing jointly.

eligibility_rules.work_requirements: Must have earned income from employment or self-employment
not_eligible_if: claimed as dependent on someone else's return

application.where: IRS Free File at irs.gov or a VITA (Volunteer Income Tax Assistance) site near you
application.note: File a federal tax return even if income is too low to owe taxes — the credit is refundable

required_documents: Social Security numbers for yourself, spouse, and all qualifying children
Bank account information for direct deposit

programs.name: Child Tax Credit (CTC)
programs.full_name: Child Tax Credit / Additional Child Tax Credit
programs.description: Up to $2,000 per qualifying child under 17. Up to $1,700 is refundable (Additional CTC) for families who owe little or no taxes.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Monthly equivalent of annual credit ($2,000 per child ÷ 12 ≈ $167/child). Refundable portion up to $1,700/child.

eligibility_rules.who: Families with qualifying children under 17 at end of tax year

income.note: Full credit phases out above $200,000 (single) or $400,000 (married filing jointly). Refundable portion available at lower incomes.

application.where: IRS Free File at irs.gov or a VITA site near you

required_documents: Social Security numbers for each qualifying child
Proof that child lived with you for more than half the year

programs.name: Free / Reduced School Meals (NSLP)
programs.full_name: National School Lunch Program and School Breakfast Program
programs.description: Free or reduced-price breakfast and lunch for school-age children at participating schools. Free at ≤130% FPL; reduced price at 130–185% FPL.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Free lunch (~$3.50/day) and breakfast (~$2.00/day) × ~180 school days per year ≈ $83/month per child. Reduced price meals cost $0.40 (lunch) and $0.30 (breakfast). Categorical eligibility if household receives SNAP, TANF, or FDPIR.

income.note: Free meals at ≤130% FPL. Reduced-price meals at 130–185% FPL.

eligibility_rules.categorical_eligibility: Automatic free meals for children in households receiving SNAP, TANF, or FDPIR — no separate application required

application.where: Child's school — submit the household income form at the start of each school year
application.processing_time_days: Typically within a few days of application

required_documents: Completed household income application (available from child's school)
Or: proof of SNAP, TANF, or FDPIR participation (for categorical eligibility)

programs.name: Child Care Subsidy (CCDF)
programs.description: Subsidizes child care costs for low-income working families with children under 13. Covers most or all of the cost of licensed day care, after-school care, or family child care.

monthly_benefit_range.note: Varies widely by state, income, age of child, and type of care. Family pays a small sliding-scale copay; subsidy covers the rest.

eligibility_rules.who: Families with children under 13 (or under 19 if disabled)

income.note: Typically ≤85% of state median income. Limits and copay amounts vary by state.

eligibility_rules.work_requirements: Parent or guardian must be working, in school, or in job training
eligibility_rules.priority: Families with children in foster care, children with disabilities, and families with very low incomes often get priority

application.where: State child care subsidy office — find at childcare.gov
application.processing_time_days: Varies; many states have wait lists — apply as early as possible
application.note: Wait lists are common. Apply even if you expect a wait.

required_documents: Proof of income for all working adults (pay stubs, employer letter)
Proof of employment, school enrollment, or job training
Child care provider name, address, and license number

programs.name: Medicare Savings Programs
programs.full_name: Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI, QDWI)
programs.description: Helps people with Medicare pay for premiums, deductibles, and copays. QMB (most generous) covers Part A and B premiums plus all cost-sharing. SLMB and QI cover the Part B premium (~$185/month).

monthly_benefit_range.note: QMB saves roughly $185/month (Part B premium) plus cost-sharing on Medicare services. SLMB and QI save the Part B premium. QMB also eliminates Medicare copays and deductibles.

who: aged 65+, or under 65 with qualifying disability

income.note: QMB: individual ~$1,275/month (~100% FPL), couple ~$1,724/month. SLMB: individual ~$1,526/month (~120% FPL). QI: individual ~$1,715/month (~135% FPL). 2026 guidelines; states may use slightly higher limits.

assets.note: Asset limits apply but are generous: $9,660 individual / $14,470 couple (2026). Many assets excluded.

not_eligible_if: not enrolled in Medicare Part A

application.where: State Medicaid office (not Social Security). Medicare.gov has a tool to find your state office.
application.note: Also ask about the Extra Help / Low Income Subsidy program for Medicare prescription drug costs — similar income limits

required_documents: Proof of income (Social Security award letter, pay stubs)
